title: Senior Corps Volunteer Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Senior Corps Volunteer Programs 5 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS AMERICORPS OFFICE PENDLETON SENIOR COMPANION AND FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides volunteer opportunities. Participants can volunteer with the Foster Grandparent Program or Senior Companion Program. Foster Grandparent Program engages low income seniors age 55 and older in providing one-on-one mentoring and academic support to children with special or exceptional needs. Senior Companion Program engages low income seniors age 55 and older in providing supportive, individualized services to help homebound seniors and other adults maintain their dignity and independence. 221 S Main Street Suite 4 Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Age 55 and older. Must be able to pass a background check and meet income guidelines. Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 276-6074 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a volunteer opportunity for older adults to connect with children and youth. Volunteers receive a stipend for each hour of service as well as transportation reimbursement, paid time off, training, accident, personal liability, and excess automobile liability insurance as well as community recognition. The stipend is tax-free and is non-declarable and will not affect any other State or Federal benefits of participants. 1 West Main Street Suite 303 Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: To volunteer: -Age 55 and older and in good health -Ability to pass State and FBI background checks -Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: No standard hours, volunteer service hours 5-40 hours per week Email (541) 857-7786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK RSVP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers volunteer opportunities. Participants receive supplemental insurance while on duty and may be eligible for mileage reimbursement to their volunteer site. Volunteer opportunities service opportunities that match their interests, skills, and schedules, including peer counseling and providing rides. 1 West Main Street Suite 303 Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 55 and older, must pass a criminal history background check Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 857-7780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PACIFIC RETIREMENT SERVICES INC COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster Grandparents and Retired and Senior Volunteer Program (RSVP) offer opportunities to earn supplemental income (non-taxable stipend) and to help the community providing supports to older adults and children. 1 W Main Street Suite 303 Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 646-3300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RSVP OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES AMERICORPS SENIORS OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES ALBANY OFFICE RETIRED AND SENIOR VOLUNTEER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers volunteer opportunities. Volunteers choose how, where, and how often they want to serve. They receive supplemental insurance while on duty and may be eligible for mileage reimbursement to their volunteer site. Volunteer opportunities include food pantries, advocacy, Medicare counseling, money management, meal delivery, reading with youth, tax preparation, cleaners, and more. 1400 Queen Avenue SE Suite 201 Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Age 55 and older, must pass criminal history background check Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 967-8630 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RSVP OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES AMERICORPS SENIORS OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES TOLEDO OFFICE RETIRED AND SENIOR VOLUNTEER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers volunteer opportunities. Volunteers choose how, where, and how often they want to serve. They receive supplemental insurance while on duty and may be eligible for mileage reimbursement to their volunteer site. Volunteer opportunities include food pantries, advocacy, Medicare counseling, money management, meal delivery, reading with youth, tax preparation, cleaners, and more. 203 N Main Street Toledo, OR 97391 Eligibility: Age 55 and older, must pass criminal history background check Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 336-2289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS SENIOR COMPANION AND FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM PACIFIC RETIREMENT SERVICES INC COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK RSVP OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES RETIRED AND SENIOR VOLUNTEER PROGRAM RSVP OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES RETIRED AND SENIOR VOLUNTEER PROGRAM
